rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 18, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Cardone Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Alberigi was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs¿ responses identified Frank Gadayan as a person with knowledge supporting plaintiffs¿ claims against defendant and defendant failed to demonstrate that he did not have such knowledge. Mr. Gadayan was deposed in this case but defendant failed to appear at that deposition and ask questions about its products. (See Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Construction Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64.) As to the negation of an element prong of Aguilar, defendant also failed to sustain its burden of production. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Mr. McClave¿s declaration based on CRC 3.1115 and relevance are sustained. The court only considered this as a motion for summary judgment. Although defendant¿s caption titles it a motion for summary judgment or in the alternative motion for summary adjudication, defendant¿s notice and separate statement failed to state the specific causes of action or issues sought to be adjudicated in violation of CRC 3.1350(b), (d). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH KOSICH et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 18, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant The Budd Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Kosich was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The court did not consider defendant¿s late-filed evidence. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
